name: Protected-Trait Proxy Detector
description: Automatically detects when hiring decisions might be influenced by protected characteristics like age, race, or gender, helping prevent illegal discrimination.

name: Human Decision Gate on All Consequential Actions
description: Requires humans to review and approve all significant hiring decisions, ensuring AI doesn't make critical choices without human oversight.

name: Unified ATS + CRM + scheduling + analytics on a single data model
description: Combines candidate profiles, recruiter conversations, interview scheduling, and hiring analytics in one unified system for seamless workflow.

name: End-to-End Requisition Orchestration
description: Automates the entire hiring process from job posting through offer, coordinating all steps and tracking progress end-to-end.

name: Secure Tool Router with Safe Action Semantics
description: Routes candidate data securely through systems with strict controls, preventing unauthorized access or misuse of sensitive information.

name: Consent First Data Minimization Orchestrator
description: Ensures only necessary candidate information is collected and processed with explicit consent, minimizing data exposure.

name: API Level Data Residency and Tenant Isolation
description: Isolates each company's hiring data separately while enforcing data location rules, preventing cross-tenant leaks.

name: Sensitive Data Segregation Vault
description: Stores highly sensitive data like SSNs and background check results in a separate secure vault with restricted access.

name: Retention and Deletion Orchestrator
description: Automatically deletes candidate records according to legal retention policies, ensuring compliance with privacy regulations.

name: Fine-Grained Access and Just-in-Time Review
description: Controls who can access candidate data and requires real-time approval for sensitive information access, creating audit trails.

name: Prompt Injection and Output Safety Firewall
description: Protects against malicious attempts to manipulate AI decisions through prompt injection, ensuring AI integrity.

name: Enterprise-grade security architecture with zero-trust design
description: Implements defense-in-depth security architecture where every system is verified and nothing is assumed safe.

name: Agent and Model Governance Console
description: Gives administrators centralized control over which AI models and agents are approved for use in hiring.

name: Explainable Hiring Decisions Layer
description: Provides transparent explanations for AI hiring recommendations, helping recruiters understand why candidates are ranked a certain way.

name: Explainability Ledger for Every AI Suggestion
description: Creates a detailed log of every suggestion made by AI, showing reasoning and evidence for each recommendation.

name: Job-Criterion Trace Map
description: Maps which job requirements drove each AI decision, showing if the decision was based on relevant qualifications.

name: Reason-Code Compiler
description: Compiles plain-English reasons for every hiring decision that can be shared with candidates or used in compliance audits.

name: Prompt-to-Action Transparency Panel
description: Shows recruiters exactly how AI interpreted the hiring instructions and which factors it weighted most heavily.

name: Embedded Model and Prompt Cards
description: Displays the AI model, version, and exact prompt used for each decision, creating transparency and accountability.

name: Hiring AI Model Registry
description: Maintains a catalog of all AI models used in hiring with their approval status and deployment history.

name: Approved-Model Control Plane
description: Controls which approved AI models can be used in live hiring, preventing unapproved or outdated models from running.

name: Secure Shadow Evaluation Before Release
description: Tests new AI models in production-like conditions before releasing them to recruiters, catching problems early.

name: Policy-Bound Model Catalog
description: Ensures every AI model used in hiring complies with company policies and legal requirements throughout its lifecycle.

name: Human-Readable Decision Summaries
description: Converts technical AI reasoning into clear summaries that non-technical people like hiring managers can easily understand.

name: Role-Adaptive Explainability Views
description: Customizes AI explanations for different users: detailed data for compliance teams, simple summaries for recruiters.

name: Explainability Quality Metrics Dashboard
description: Tracks how well AI explanations help human decision-makers understand and trust AI recommendations.

name: AI Use Disclosure and Mode Labeling
description: Clearly labels when AI is being used in hiring and shows the mode, helping candidates understand what's analyzing them.

name: Data Provenance And Training Set Registry
description: Documents where all training data came from and maintains a searchable registry of what trained each AI model.

name: Governance‑Bound Change Management For Models
description: Requires formal approval and documentation for all changes to AI models, preventing unauthorized modifications.

name: AI Model Versioning and Rollback
description: Tracks every version of an AI model and allows rolling back to previous versions if problems are discovered.

name: Real-Time Explainability Dashboard (Agentic Flow Visualization)
description: Displays a real-time visual dashboard showing how AI and humans are working through the hiring workflow.

name: Per-decision explainable rationale
description: Provides a clear reason statement for each individual hiring decision that can be shared with candidates.

name: Pre-Deployment Bias Testing Suite
description: Tests AI models before deployment to ensure they don't discriminate against protected groups.

name: Natural Language Decision Justification
description: Generates natural explanations for hiring decisions using conversational language instead of technical jargon.

name: Agentic Model Governance & Explainability
description: Ensures AI agents used in hiring can be governed and understood, with full visibility into their reasoning.

name: Explainable AI Ranking
description: Ranks candidates while showing step-by-step reasoning for why one candidate scored higher than another.

name: Explainable Decision Surfaces
description: Visualizes the decision-making surface showing how different qualifications affect candidate scores.

name: Continuous Model Drift Monitoring
description: Continuously monitors AI performance to catch when models start producing worse results over time.

name: Agentic Action Transparency Log
description: Creates a log of every action an AI takes in hiring, including what data it accessed and what it decided.

name: AI Confidence Banding with Uncertainty Display
description: Shows how confident the AI is in each recommendation with visual indicators of uncertainty.

name: Counterfactual Rejection Analyzer
description: Analyzes rejected candidates to understand what factors led to rejection and if patterns seem fair.

name: Human-Readable AI Narrative Summaries
description: Summarizes AI reasoning into natural language narratives that explain hiring decisions to non-technical audiences.

name: Model Version Snapshot Binding
description: Locks hiring decisions to the specific AI version that made them, preventing retroactive changes to explanations.

name: AI Override with Accountability Trail
description: Allows humans to override AI decisions while recording why they did so and by whom, creating accountability.

name: Escalation-Required Edge Cases
description: Automatically flags unusual or high-risk decisions that require human escalation and review before proceeding.

name: Model Rollback and Emergency Freeze Controls
description: Enables quick freezing of an AI model across the system if problems are detected, with fast rollback options.

name: Model Card Generation and Disclosure
description: Auto-generates detailed documentation about AI models including what they do, their limitations, and performance metrics.

name: Audit-Ready Model Versioning
description: Maintains a complete audit history of every AI model version ever used in hiring with deployment dates.

name: Mandatory Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoints
description: Requires mandatory human review at specific points in the hiring workflow, preventing AI-only decisions.

name: Consent-Based AI Model Training
description: Only trains AI models using candidate data from those who explicitly consented to having their data used.

name: Model Card + Versioned Prompt Registry
description: Maintains a searchable registry of all AI models and the exact prompts used, versioned for audit compliance.

name: Prompt-Level Human Approval Gate for High-Risk Agents
description: Requires human approval before high-risk AI agents can execute decisions like rejecting candidates.

name: Protected-Attribute Redaction Engine Before Inference
description: Removes protected attributes like age and race from candidate profiles before AI sees them, preventing bias.

name: Configurable Risk-Threshold Human Approval Gates
description: Allows companies to set custom risk thresholds that trigger human approval for decisions above that threshold.

name: Model Card Automation
description: Automatically creates and maintains updated model documentation without manual effort required.

name: Explainable AI with transparent reasoning chains for every recommendation
description: Shows every reasoning step the AI took to reach a recommendation, creating a transparent chain of logic.

name: Recruiter Agentic Co-Pilot
description: An AI assistant that helps recruiters by drafting emails, summarizing candidates, and automating routine hiring tasks.

name: Event Sourced Hiring Ledger
description: Records every hiring event and decision in an immutable ledger for compliance audits and dispute resolution.

name: Org-Wide Hiring Health Dashboard (Agent-Curated)
description: A centralized dashboard showing hiring health metrics, bottlenecks, and pace across the entire organization.

name: Fairness Metrics Benchmarking
description: Compares hiring fairness metrics across teams and roles to identify where bias problems might be emerging.

name: Cross-System Decision Correlation
description: Analyzes whether different systems are making inconsistent decisions for similar candidates.

name: Automated Requisition Velocity Monitoring with Bottleneck Detection
description: Automatically identifies and alerts on hiring bottlenecks like interview stages taking too long.

name: Automated Requisition Health Scoring with Intervention Playbooks
description: Scores each job requisition's health and suggests specific actions to improve hiring speed and quality.

name: Dynamic Bottleneck Detection & Recovery
description: Detects hiring slowdowns in real-time and suggests recovery actions to get back on track.

name: Real-Time Process Telemetry Dashboard
description: A live dashboard displaying real-time metrics on hiring velocity, completion rates, and process health.

name: Immutable, Tamper-Evident Audit Logs
description: Creates audit logs that cannot be altered after creation, proving that hiring decisions were made fairly.

name: Automated Bias Audit Scheduling
description: Automatically schedules bias audits at regular intervals to ensure ongoing fairness in hiring.

name: Adverse Impact Analysis by Stage
description: Analyzes whether hiring is having disparate impact on protected groups at each interview stage.

name: Override and Justification Logging
description: Logs every time a recruiter overrides an AI decision and documents their reason for the override.

name: Cross-functional analytics dashboards for finance, executives, and TA ops
description: Provides role-specific dashboards for finance, executives, and talent ops teams with their most relevant metrics.

name: Real-Time Pipeline Health Dashboard
description: Displays current pipeline status in real-time with visibility into stage progression and potential problems.

name: Conversational Candidate Concierge (Core feature)
description: An AI chatbot that candidates can talk to about their application, interview schedule, and next steps 24/7.

name: Multilingual Experience Agent (Premium differentiator)
description: Communicates with candidates in their preferred language and understands cultural context of different regions.

name: USP: 24/7 Concierge Agent
description: A round-the-clock AI concierge that answers candidate questions anytime, improving experience and reducing recruiter load.

name: Adaptive Application Experience
description: Customizes the application experience for each candidate based on their background and the role requirements.

name: Multi-Lingual, Region-Aware Candidate Agent
description: Conversations with candidates are conducted in their language and adjusted for regional hiring norms.

name: Candidate AI Use Passport
description: Candidates receive a document showing which AI was used to evaluate them and how they can request explanation.

name: Candidate Explanation and Appeal Center
description: A dedicated portal where rejected candidates can get explanations and appeal hiring decisions.

name: Transparency-by-Default Interviewing
description: Interview processes are conducted with full transparency about when AI is evaluating and what criteria matters.

name: Candidate Feedback And Appeal Channel
description: Candidates can easily provide feedback on their experience and appeal decisions through a simple channel.

name: "Why Was I Rejected?" Candidate Report
description: Rejected candidates receive a clear report explaining the key reasons they weren't selected.

name: Transparent Application Progress Tracker
description: Candidates can track their application status in real-time and see where they are in the interview process.

name: Candidate Rights Fulfillment Portal
description: Centralized portal where candidates can request their data, privacy rights fulfillment, and other data access requests.

name: Candidate AI Transparency Notice Engine
description: Automatically sends candidates clear notices when AI is evaluating them and what that means for their application.

name: Candidate Data Access and Redress Portal
description: Allows candidates to download their data, request corrections, and understand how their information is being used.

name: One-Click Appeal to Human Reviewer
description: Rejected candidates can click once to escalate their case to a human reviewer for a second opinion.

name: Candidate-centric scheduling that optimizes their experience, not just internal efficiency
description: Interview scheduling considers candidate availability and preferences first, not just internal team convenience.

name: Conversational application that eliminates forms entirely for appropriate roles
description: Candidates apply through natural conversation instead of filling out long forms, improving experience and completion rates.

name: Real-time transparent status tracking with timeline predictions
description: Shows candidates real-time status updates with predictions of when they can expect to hear back next.

name: Personalized, multi-channel candidate engagement throughout the lifecycle
description: Connects with candidates through email, SMS, and in-app messages at every stage, keeping them informed and engaged throughout the hiring process.

name: Automated Requisition-Closure Notifier
description: Automatically notifies candidates when a job position closes, providing transparency and reducing confusion about application status.

name: Candidate Right-to-Reply Portal
description: Allows rejected candidates to submit a formal response or appeal regarding their rejection decision.

name: Candidate Appeal Workflow
description: Provides a structured workflow for candidates to formally challenge hiring decisions, ensuring fair reconsideration opportunities.

name: Policy as Code Hiring Governance Layer
description: Codifies hiring rules and governance policies into automated system controls, ensuring consistent compliance across all hiring decisions.

name: Manager Calibration and Bias Drift Monitor
description: Tracks and alerts when hiring managers show inconsistent decision patterns or increasing bias over time, enabling early intervention.

name: Inappropriate Question and Bias Guard
description: Detects and warns against legally risky or biased interview questions before they're asked, protecting the company from compliance violations.

name: Fairness and Adverse Impact Copilot with Counterfactual What If
description: Analyzes hiring decisions for fairness and shows what-if scenarios to prevent discriminatory outcomes before they impact candidates.

name: Audit Pack Generator for Decisions
description: Generates comprehensive documentation packages to legally defend hiring decisions if challenged in disputes or audits.

name: Global Multi Entity Hiring Rules Engine
description: Applies consistent hiring rules across multiple countries and legal jurisdictions, ensuring global compliance with local employment laws.

name: Explainable AI Compliance Agent (Category-defining bet)
description: Provides clear, understandable explanations for AI-driven hiring recommendations, ensuring transparency and legal defensibility.

name: Bias Mitigation Agent
description: Automatically identifies and reduces unfair patterns in how AI models rate or rank candidates across demographic groups.

name: Autonomous Candidate Fraud Detector
description: Scans applications for forged credentials, falsified experience, or identity fraud to protect hiring integrity.

name: Geopatriation Data Residency Router
description: Routes candidate data storage to comply with regional privacy laws, ensuring data stays in the correct geographic location.

name: NYC Local Law 144 Bias Auditor
description: Ensures hiring practices comply with NYC's pay transparency and bias audit requirements for local operations.

name: PII Redaction & Blind Hiring Enforcer
description: Automatically removes personal identifying information from applications to enable fair, unbiased blind hiring reviews.

name: Worker Council (Betriebsrat) Compliance Agent
description: Ensures all hiring decisions comply with German works council requirements and employee consultation obligations.

name: Applicant Fraud and Identity Verification Neutralizer
description: Verifies candidate identity and screens for misrepresentation to prevent hiring fraud and ensure legitimate applicants.

name: Cross-Entity Global Compliance Guardian
description: Maintains consistent hiring compliance standards across all company divisions and countries operating under different regulations.

name: DEI Decision Auditor Agent
description: Audits hiring decisions against diversity and inclusion goals, documenting that selections were legally defensible and unbiased.

name: Bias Drift Detector
description: Continuously monitors AI models to catch when bias gradually emerges, triggering corrective action before it affects hiring.

name: Global Offer Compliance Guardian
description: Validates job offers against salary requirements, legal minimums, and local regulations for each geography before finalizing.

name: Interviewer Calibration & Drift Monitor
description: Monitors interview panel consistency and alerts when individual interviewers show rating bias or pattern drift over time.

name: DEI Fairness Simulator & Auditor
description: Simulates hiring scenarios and audits actual outcomes to identify and eliminate fairness gaps before they cause legal exposure.

name: End-to-End AI Decision Audit Trail
description: Creates complete, traceable records of every AI decision, enabling legal teams to reconstruct how each hiring choice was made.

name: Bias & Drift Monitor for Models and Workflows
description: Monitors both AI models and human workflows for emerging bias and performance drift, maintaining fairness standards continuously.

name: Confidence-Driven Escalation Thresholds
description: Routes low-confidence hiring recommendations to human review, preventing risky automated decisions from proceeding unsupervised.

name: End-to-End Hiring Evidence Vault
description: Securely stores all hiring evidence, documentation, and decision records for audit defense and regulatory compliance.

name: Immutable Decision Timeline
description: Creates permanent, unchangeable records of when each hiring decision was made and by whom, preventing tampering or rewriting history.

name: Audit Replay Mode
description: Allows legal and compliance teams to replay and examine past hiring decisions as they existed at any point in time.

name: Versioned Requisition Snapshotting
description: Preserves snapshots of job requirements and criteria as they change over time, documenting the basis for each hiring decision.

name: AI Change Impact Diff
description: Tracks exactly what changed when AI systems or decision rules are updated, documenting impact on candidate outcomes.

name: Litigation Hold for Hiring Records
description: Preserves all hiring records indefinitely when litigation is threatened, ensuring evidence cannot be inadvertently deleted.

name: Counsel and Procurement Evidence Export
description: Exports hiring evidence packages in formats legal counsel and procurement auditors require for disputes or investigations.

name: Stage-Level Adverse Impact Monitor
description: Monitors hiring stages for discrimination patterns that unfairly eliminate candidates based on protected characteristics.

name: Pre-Go-Live Fairness Gate
description: Blocks deployment of hiring tools until fairness testing confirms no unacceptable bias or discrimination exists.

name: Criteria Health Check for Job Relevance
description: Verifies that job selection criteria directly match job requirements, ensuring hiring is based on relevant qualifications only.

name: Drift and Feedback-Loop Monitor
description: Detects when feedback loops or previous hiring patterns begin unfairly influencing current candidate evaluations.

name: Accommodation-Safe Alternative Flow Engine
description: Ensures candidates with disabilities receive accommodations during hiring while maintaining fair evaluation processes.

name: Risk-Based Approval Chains for AI Automation
description: Routes high-risk hiring decisions through stricter approval chains to ensure proper oversight and reduce errors.

name: Two-Person Review for High-Risk Filters
description: Requires two independent reviewers to approve high-risk filtering criteria before they eliminate candidates from consideration.

name: Override with Mandatory Reason Capture
description: Requires hiring managers to document reasons when they override AI recommendations, creating accountability and audit trails.

name: AI Recommendation Quarantine Queue
description: Holds AI-generated candidate recommendations in a review queue before they affect decisions, allowing human verification first.

name: Policy-Aware Agent Boundaries
description: Configures AI agents to stay within defined policy boundaries and cannot recommend actions that violate company hiring rules.

name: Jurisdiction-Aware Hiring Policy Engine
description: Automatically applies different hiring rules based on the specific country and laws where each position is located.

name: Built-In DPIA and Impact Assessment Workspace
description: Provides built-in templates and tools to complete privacy impact assessments required by data protection regulations.

name: Works Council and Consultation Packager
description: Packages hiring information and decisions in formats required for worker council reviews and regulatory consultations.

name: Aligned-Criteria Library (Job-Relatedness Guardrails)

name: Immutable Hiring Decision Ledger
description: Records every hiring decision in an immutable ledger that cannot be altered, ensuring permanent auditability.

name: Standardized Audit Export Toolkit
description: Provides standardized exports of hiring evidence and audit data in formats regulators, courts, and auditors expect.

name: AI Audit Readiness Scorecard
description: Scores how well hiring systems meet legal compliance requirements, identifying gaps before audits uncover them.

name: Continuous Impact Assessment Engine
description: Continuously measures hiring impact across demographics to catch discrimination emerging in real-time, not retroactively.

name: Configurable Fairness Objectives Per Role

name: Adaptive Bias Monitoring With Drift Detection
description: Adapts bias monitoring as hiring patterns evolve, automatically detecting emerging discrimination without manual reconfiguration.

name: Candidate Demographic Voluntariness And Segregated Use
description: Ensures candidates freely choose to share demographic data and that information is used only for compliance, not decision-making.

name: Human‑Final Decision Enforcement

name: Escalation Paths For High‑Risk Decisions

name: Oversight Workbench For Compliance And Legal
description: Provides a dedicated workbench for compliance and legal teams to oversee hiring decisions and investigate concerns.

name: Structured Human Justification Capture

name: Granular AI Feature Consent Management

name: Sensitive Modality Governance (Video/Voice/Psychometrics)

name: Jurisdiction‑Aware Workflow Engine
description: Routes hiring workflows differently based on location laws, automatically adapting processes for each jurisdiction.

name: Regulation Template Library And Playbooks
description: Provides pre-built templates and playbooks for complying with hiring regulations in different countries and regions.

name: Compliance Change Alerting And Simulation
description: Alerts hiring teams when new laws emerge and simulates how changes would impact current hiring practices.

name: Immutable "Decision Chain of Custody"

name: AI Interaction Audit Logs (Not Just User Logs)

name: Real-Time Adverse Impact Monitoring
description: Monitors hiring in real-time to immediately flag discrimination patterns that would violate employment law.

name: Mandatory Human Review Gate (MRG)

name: "Human Override" Attribution
description: Documents exactly which hiring decisions humans overrode and why, creating accountability and audit evidence.

name: Two-Person Rule for Model Deployment
description: Requires two separate approvers before any AI model or hiring algorithm can be deployed to live decisions.

name: Granular, Per-Model Consent Management

name: Jurisdiction-Specific Workflow Automation
description: Automatically customizes hiring workflows and rules based on the specific country and legal requirements of each position.

name: Automated OFCCP Reporting Suite

name: GDPR Article 22 "Significant Decision" Flagging

name: EU AI Act Risk Tiering

name: "Opt-Out of AI" Path

name: Immutable Tool-Call Provenance Ledger
description: Records exactly which AI tools and systems were used in each hiring decision, creating full transparency and traceability.

name: Six-Year Retained Compliance Archive

name: Automated EEOC/Title VII Audit Extractor

name: Policy Evaluation Reconstructive Search

name: Time-Stamped Decision Boundary Snapshots

name: Knockout Filter Disparate Impact Simulator

name: Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Forced Justification Prompt

name: EU AI Act Annex III Risk Classifier

name: DPIA Pre-Filled Compliance Exporter

name: Multi-Jurisdictional AI Notice Automator

name: EU AI Act auto-conformity package

name: Annual bias-audit automation

name: Real-time Bias Detection Dashboard
description: Monitors hiring patterns in real-time to spot and alert teams to potential bias before it affects decisions.

name: Automated Evidence Pack Generation

name: Configurable Human-in-the-Loop Triggers

name: End-to-End Process Provenance

name: Continuous Fairness Monitoring

name: Consent Versioning and Tracking

name: Override Pattern Analysis

name: Dynamic Human-in-the-loop Decisioning with Explainability Surfaces

name: Dynamic Human-in-the-loop Decisioning

name: Global Entity-Aware Compliance Engine

name: Real-time adverse impact monitoring at every pipeline stage

name: Jurisdiction-aware compliance guardrails that adapt automatically

name: Anonymous Shortlisting

name: Interview Consistency & Drift Monitor

name: Bias-Audited Rubric Generator

name: Blind Screening Automation

name: Prompt Injection Defense Shield

name: Data Retention Schedules for AI Inputs

name: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) for AI Audit Logs

name: SOC 2 Type II for AI Services

name: PII/PHI Real-Time Masking Layer

name: Granular Consent Context Manager

name: Zero-Trust Model Pipeline Validator

name: Consent-Driven Data Processing

name: Automatic Right-to-Be-Forgotten Workflow
